content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: December 15, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Ted Prim

Subject: Letter to Bill Schwartz, San Diego County Counsel

The justification for our conclusion in the Schwartz letter is based on Section 87307. That section provides that any person can initiate an amendment to an agency's code. If the amendment is not adopted, that person may appeal the agency's decision to the code reviewing body who may deny the request or order the agency to adopt the amendment. Thus, in effect, a member of the code reviewing body could initiate the amendment and if the agency did not comply, the code reviewing could mandate the amendment.

By permitting the code reviewing body as an agency to initiate an amendment and hold one hearing, paperwork is reduced and the time of agency employees is saved.

TP:nrw
